claim: Iran's IRGC seized two of three cargo ships in the Strait of Hormuz on April 22, 2026, escorting them to Iranian waters for alleged maritime violations.
US President Trump extended a ceasefire on April 22, 2026, while maintaining a port blockade on Iran.
Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf declared on April 23, 2026, that reopening the Strait of Hormuz is 'impossible' under US blockade conditions.
The US Treasury imposed sanctions on April 22-23, 2026, targeting 14 entities in Iran, Turkey, and UAE aiding Iran's ballistic missile and Shahed drone programs.
Trump indicated possible peace talks in 36-72 hours via Pakistan on April 22-23, 2026, but Iranian officials insisted on ending the blockade first.
The US halted approximately $500 million in dollar shipments to Iraq on April 22, 2026, and froze security ties to counter Iran-backed militias.
Iran claims rebuilt military forces to approximately 70% pre-war strength with thousands of ballistic missiles in reserve at underground sites, according to US intelligence assessments.
Tehran signaled it has 'new cards to play' if war resumes on April 21, 2026, amid talks uncertainty.
Exiled Prince Reza Pahlavi addressed reporters in Berlin, calling Iranian negotiators 'different faces of the same machine.'
Dozens of Iranian security forces were arrested in Kermanshah for refusing to fire on protesters, indicating internal dissent and defection concerns.
Supreme Leader Khamenei ordered a 'crush by any means necessary' crackdown on protesters on January 9, 2026.
Iran experienced an internet blackout lasting 55 days as of the reporting period, with efforts to confiscate Starlink dishes ongoing.
Iran's economy is buckling under war, sanctions, US blockade, and global pressures at a faster rate than the US or global economy.
Foreign militias, including 800-5,000 Iranian-backed Iraqi fighters, were deployed to Iran to assist with suppressing protests.
State media claimed 87% public support for war in Iran, a claim that sparked public ridicule.
Iran's judiciary chief hailed IRGC actions in the Strait of Hormuz following vessel seizures.
Supreme National Security Council Secretary Larijani met with Russian President Putin in Moscow in recent meetings.
LEC Commander Radan met with the PRC ambassador to discuss a security deal.
